title: Antimima rostella
taxonomy: Antimima rostella is classified within the family Mesembryanthemaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as ice plants or dune plants. This family belongs to the order Caryophyllales, which is a large order of flowering plants that also includes carnations, pinks, and sandworts. The Mesembryanthemaceae family is further divided into several genera, with Antimima being one of them. The classification of Antimima rostella places it within the realm of succulent plants, which are adapted to store water in their leaves, stems, or roots. Through this classification, we can see that Antimima rostella shares characteristics with other succulent plants in its ability to thrive in dry environments.
